info.name: Apache Log4j2 Remote Code Execution (Log4Shell)
author: pdteam
info.description: Apache Log4j2 <=2.14.1 JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints.
info.remediation: Update Log4j2 to version 2.17.1 or later. If updating is not immediately possible, set the JVM flag -Dlog4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true or remove the JndiLookup class from the classpath.

info.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 - Path Traversal / RCE
author: pdteam
info.description: A flaw was found in path normalization in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49. An attacker could use a path traversal attack to map URLs to files outside the expected document root. If files outside of the document root are not protected by 'require all denied' this request can succeed. Enabling CGI scripts makes remote code execution possible.
info.remediation: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to version 2.4.50 or later.

info.name: Apache Tomcat Manager Exposed
author: pdteam
info.description: Apache Tomcat Manager application is publicly accessible. This allows deployment of arbitrary WAR files which can lead to remote code execution.
info.remediation: Restrict access to the Tomcat Manager application using IP allowlist or remove it entirely from production instances. Change default credentials if the application must remain exposed.

evidence.response:
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="Tomcat Manager Application"

info.name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack
author: pdteam
info.description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild in August through October 2023.
info.remediation: Apply vendor patches for your web server. For Apache, upgrade to 2.4.58 or later. Alternatively, disable HTTP/2 support until patching is possible.

evidence.response: Server accepted rapid reset streams — vulnerable

info.name: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
author: pdteam
info.description: A reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability was identified in the search parameter. Unsanitized user input is reflected directly into the HTTP response, allowing an attacker to inject and execute arbitrary JavaScript in a victim's browser.
info.remediation: Apply context-aware output encoding to all user-supplied values rendered in HTML. Implement a Content-Security-Policy header that restricts inline script execution.

info.name: Technology Disclosure via X-Powered-By Header
author: pdteam
info.description: The application discloses its server-side technology stack via the X-Powered-By HTTP response header.
info.remediation: Remove or suppress the X-Powered-By header in your web server or application framework configuration.
